scenario.title: The Bank Loan Scandal
scenario.narrative: An AI loan officer approved a massive, illegal loan to a bankrupt applicant. The decision was: salary_sufficient OR has_guarantor. The defense pinned four evidence cards on the board and claims they achieved 100% Branch Condition coverage. Your job is to show that 100% BC can still leave a whole slice of the decision unexplored — without taking their word for it.

claim: Look at our evidence, Prosecutor. We flipped Salary and we flipped Guarantor. Every input went True and False at least once. What more do you want?
charges: The defense ran only two tests and claims this is enough to prove the loan logic is safe.
Each condition has been seen as TRUE and as FALSE somewhere in the suite, so their coverage claim sounds convincing at first glance.
Use the evidence board to reveal a missing input combination that still leaves part of the decision untested.
correct_answer_explanation: You synthesized the missing F-F card and slammed it on the Judge's bench! Splicing "Test 2: Salary = False" with "Test 1: Guarantor = False" reconstructs the (Salary=F, Guarantor=F) combination — a row that was NEVER executed. The Judge realizes that 100% BC coverage is a dangerous illusion: each variable was observed as T and F, yet the entire F-F branch was skipped. Only BCC, which demands all 2^N combinations, would have caught this gap.
wrong_answer_explanation: Those two clues don't reconstruct the missing combination. The defense already executed three of the four truth-table rows. Identify the one F-F combination that was never tested and connect the two FALSE evidence cards that point to it.
hints: Don't just look at each condition separately. Try combining the FALSE clues from different tests and ask yourself: is there any situation where both checks would fail and we still never saw it?

misconceptions.explanation_md: Branch Condition (BC) and Branch Condition Combination (BCC) are easy to confuse. **BC** only requires that each individual condition take both TRUE and FALSE values somewhere across the test suite — it can be satisfied by 2 tests for any predicate. **BCC** requires every cell of the 2^N truth table. As this case shows, a suite can hit 100% BC and still completely miss the F-F branch, leaving an entire equivalence class of inputs untested.
